title: UserApprovalTaskDefinition
description: Defines a Task for a given User to approve.

displayName.title: Display Name
displayName.description: Display Name that identifies this Node.

addReviewers.description: Add the Reviewers to the assignees List.

addOwners.description: Add the Owners to the assignees List.

candidates.description: List of specific candidates (users or teams) assigned to this task.

emptyAssigneeStrategy.description: Strategy applied when no reviewers, owners, or candidates resolve to assignees. 'none' keeps the default behavior (the gateway auto-approves event-driven approvals and leaves workflow-managed tasks unassigned); 'assignAdmins' falls back to all platform admins, excluding the requester so self-approval can never happen.

approvalThreshold.title: Approval Threshold
approvalThreshold.description: Number of reviewers that must approve for the task to be completed. Default is 1 (any single reviewer can approve).

rejectionThreshold.title: Rejection Threshold
rejectionThreshold.description: Number of reviewers that must reject for the task to be rejected. Default is 1 (any single reviewer can reject). This allows for scenarios where you want multiple approvals but a single rejection can veto.

stageId.title: Stage Id
stageId.description: Workflow stage identifier stored on the task while this user task is active.

stageDisplayName.title: Stage Display Name
stageDisplayName.description: Human-readable stage label shown to task assignees.

taskStatus.title: Task Status
taskStatus.description: Coarse task status mapped while this user task is active.

assigneeStrategy.title: Assignee Strategy
assigneeStrategy.description: Optional label describing how assignees are derived for the stage.

transitionMetadata.title: Transition Metadata
transitionMetadata.description: Transitions available from this stage. Edge conditions should match these transition ids.

expiryTimer.title: Expiry Timer
expiryTimer.description: Auto-fires after the ISO 8601 duration in the named process variable elapses. The boundary timer interrupts the user task and exits the subprocess with the configured transitionId as the node's result, so an outgoing edge with that condition routes the workflow downstream (e.g. to auto-revoke or auto-close).

durationVariable.description: Name of the process variable holding the ISO 8601 duration (e.g. 'accessDuration' → 'P14D').

transitionId.description: Result value emitted when the timer fires. Must match an outgoing edge condition from this node.

closeAsResolution.title: Close As Resolution
closeAsResolution.description: When set, the underlying Task entity is closed at the moment the timer fires with this resolutionType (the final taskStatus is derived from it by TaskRepository — TimedOut maps to Expired). Leave unset when a downstream node is responsible for closing the Task (avoids double-resolve).
